programmes.show_description: How do billions of bees produce tonnes of California almonds? How do they clear the snow to keep Norway's planes in action? How are tricky stains removed? S9, Ep5
How is a modern fighter jet built? How do Belgium's Trappist monks make the world's finest beers? How do they build America's best performing SUV? S9, Ep6
Jess's Dream VW Golf GTI. Mike Brewer and Marc 'Elvis' Priestley trade up to a VW Golf GTi for teaching assistant Jess. And the guys transform an old Fiat Doblo into a micro camper. S1, Ep5
Tom's Dream DeLorean. Mike Brewer and Marc 'Elvis' Priestley help farmer Tom trade up an old Jaguar and a tractor to a DeLorean. But with a £30,000 price tag it won't be easy. S1, Ep6
Herefordshire. Henry and Sam are in Herefordshire where they find sheds full of engines and autogyros, weird and wonderful vehicles before discovering a British classic. S1, Ep17
Gloucestershire. The team is in Gloucestershire to uncover some oily treasure. They find a picturesque farm where deer, goats and pigs mingle with vintage agricultural equipment. S1, Ep18
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as retractable saunas and sawmill blade knives made? S26, Ep16
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as plastic model kits and light microscopes made? S26, Ep17
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Furniture restorer Will is challenged to repair a hand-crafted spinning wheel. Brenton fixes a cine-projector, while Rachel revamps a rocking chair. S4, Ep10
Drew encounters thrilling examples by three design titans who shaped the classic Georgian-and-Victorian look: Sir John Soane, Augustus Pugin and William Morris. S17, Ep10
Drew visits a disused fuel shed on a former air force base in Suffolk, where he discovers vases carved from cow bones and a haunting stone face corbel. S17, Ep11
The Scrappers are robbed, threatening their chances of turning a profit. Meanwhile, the Millzilla unexpectedly breaks down, costing the Dirt Dogs $1,000 a day. S3, Ep5
One of the gold hunters unearths a monster nugget that dramatically transforms their season. Plus, the Gold Gypsies are hit by the worst storm in decades. S3, Ep6
Roo Irvine and David Harper drive through Yorkshire and County Durham, where they buy an old fishing stool in the hope of landing a big profit in Newcastle. S22, Ep2
Angus is in Sutton Coldfield to look through a life-long collection. He uncovers Beatles memorabilia and two programmes from England's 1966 World Cup win. S1, Ep2
Furniture restorer Will is challenged to repair a hand-crafted spinning wheel. Brenton fixes a cine-projector, while Rachel revamps a rocking chair. S4, Ep10
Suffolk. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk where they find a barn filled with Porsche tractors, Ducati pushbikes and many more rare and surprising vehicles. S1, Ep19
Witney. Henry and Sam head to Witney where they meet Lez, a model-builder extraordinaire, and come away with an unusual challenge. Can they make a tidy profit for Lez's trike? S1, Ep20
Martin. Henry and Fuzz visit Martin in Ipswich. Henry has his eye on a vintage Land Rover and Fuzz finds a miniature scooter, but what will Martin part with? S6, Ep3
Roger. Henry and Fuzz explore Roger's garage of buses in Coventry. For former mechanic Fuzz, it's a busman's holiday, for Henry, it's a voyage of discovery. S6, Ep4
Military Precision. A widow needs help to clear her late husband's collection of militaria. Plus, a couple hope a Chinese warlord will fund a much-needed holiday. S5, Ep23
Chocks Away! Angus helps a widow in Lincoln find new homes for her late husband's aviation paintings. Plus, he finds a ring with some serious bling in Essex. S6, Ep1
Bronze Dog And Metal Welly. Catherine Southon and Philip Serrell motor through sunny Surrey looking for antiques. With the scores finely balanced, which expert will pull ahead? S27, Ep23
100 Year Old Road Trip. With just two more auctions left on their trip, Phil Serrell pulls out all the stops as he tries to beat fellow expert Catherine Southon. S27, Ep24
Lanchester LD10. The duo explore Shane's epic collection, where a stylish Lanchester LD10 catches their eye - a perfect fit for their love of post-war British cars. S2, Ep9
American Dream. The Cogs bid for car-show glory with an iconic American pick-up truck. Richard is back on the racetrack. Can he impress the classic car power-players? S5, Ep5
Steve and Dominic are tasked with repairing a clock that stood for decades above the entrance of a newsagent's. Sonnaz works on a wooden barber's chair. S4, Ep27
How do volcanoes help produce the world's best guacamole? How do they build trucks that can cross swamps? How do they make the world-famous Fiskars scissors? S9, Ep7
How do they make steel cables strong enough to hold up a 50,000 tonne bridge? Why do modern hunters use medieval weapons? S9, Ep8
How do they make Tennessee whiskey the traditional Lincoln County way? How do night goggles work? How do the Finnish relax in 160-degree saunas? S9, Ep9
How do they grow enough vanilla to make the world's favourite ice cream flavour? How are anchors for huge ships built? How are 12 million lighters made every year? S9, Ep10
Deepak's Dream Porsche Cayenne. Car dealer supremo Mike Brewer and mechanic Marc 'Elvis' Priestley help police officer Deepak trade up his Nissan Juke to a stylish Porsche Cayenne. S1, Ep7
The Special. Mike and Elvis deliver the ultimate guide to buying a dream car, featuring auctions, car shows, auto jumbles and Mike's very own forecourt. S1, Ep8
Suffolk. Henry and Sam are in Suffolk where they find a barn filled with Porsche tractors, Ducati pushbikes and many more rare and surprising vehicles. S1, Ep19
Witney. Henry and Sam head to Witney where they meet Lez, a model-builder extraordinaire, and come away with an unusual challenge. Can they make a tidy profit for Lez's trike? S1, Ep20
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as gummy vitamins and boat trailers made? S26, Ep18
More everyday items go under the microscope, revealing their production. How are objects such as water well cylinders and nonconductive tools made? S26, Ep19
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Metal worker Dom is tasked with repairing an old barber's pole. Lucia restores a faded artwork, while Will transforms a wooden writing case. S4, Ep11
Drew and Tee visit Newark, a town renowned for its antique connections. Drew finds a set of Regency bed steps and a genuine, very rare 18th century mirror. S17, Ep12
Drew and Tee visit Insect Circus in Suffolk, where they discover a giant pair of 20th-century stage shoes and scratch-made models of fairground rides. S17, Ep13
Vern and Marko are at crisis point and Rick takes to the air in a race to peg a new lease. Plus, Alex hunts down poachers before a sunset wedding on his Quartz Hill lease. S3, Ep7
The Dirt Dogs face the fallout after Marko's shock departure, but they fight back with big tech. Plus, Rick launches a solo rescue mission for a rookie prospector. S3, Ep8
In Yorkshire, Roo learns about Victorian loos while David discovers an exciting vintage watch. Later, two brooches and a wooden cat go under the gavel in Dundee. S22, Ep3
Angus and the team clear out a big house in Whixall, Shropshire. They uncover a Victorian desk set, a hanging lantern and Vogue clothing patterns. S1, Ep3
Metal worker Dom is tasked with repairing an old barber's pole. Lucia restores a faded artwork, while Will transforms a wooden writing case. S4, Ep11
Stormy Weather, Hidden Treasure. At a wind-swept farm on a hill top in Cornwall, Henry and Sam find a collection of cars and tractors and a very rare German scooter. S2, Ep1
Hot Rods, Trucks And Miniature Bikes. In East Sussex, Henry and Sam visit a dedicated collector of American vehicles. His collection includes hot rods, trucks and other quirky items. S2, Ep2
Theo And Sarah. Henry and Fuzz meet a lifelong biker with unique motorbikes. Fuzz discovers a 1950s step through scooter, but Henry prefers low-capacity sports bikes. S6, Ep5
Adam And Dan. Henry and stand-in searcher Guy are in Wiltshire visiting collectors Adam and Dan who have an assortment of trucks, tractors, and rare American cars. S6, Ep6
Jugtastic! Angus visits a home with a pub in it, filled with over 160 jugs. Later, he encounters a colossal collection of Glen Millar Vinyl and V-discs! S6, Ep2
Desperately Needing Angus. Angus helps a woman fund her elderly mum's care, and he clears a home for a couple who went through a life-changing, traumatic event on holiday. S6, Ep3
A Superstar DJ. Catherine Southon and Phil Serrell breeze around Essex, picking up curiosities in a Mercedes convertible. Catherine enjoys a revenge prank on Phil. S27, Ep25
Antiques experts Raj Bisram and Irita Marriot hit the road in Suffolk and Norfolk. They find a 19th century automaton and a stoneware Doulton flask. S25, Ep1
A Victorian Bonanza. Angus takes a trip back in time when he visits a home that's dressed like a Victorian townhouse. Later, he helps a mother and daughter with an emotional task. S3, Ep10
Sacred Secret & Saucy Surprise. Angus sells the treasures of an Abbey to fund the monks' retirement, and a motoring fanatic's cheeky sculpture sets pulses racing on sale day. S6, Ep20
Susannah Constantine & Leslie Ash. Angus raids TV presenter Susannah's couture stash, then helps actress Leslie uncover treasures, memories, and a few famous surprises! S5, Ep4
How do they craft footballs for the National Football League? How do they farm mussels? How do they design the ultimate fighter pilot's helmet? S9, Ep11
How are powerful Scania trucks built? How do they use the world's biggest DNA database to solve crime? How is paraffin used to make Gouda cheese? S9, Ep12
